item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoting. It was strong, and had me going until the hamfisted 2747 coattail riding- well, it lost me before then. It lost me when the apparently nonexistent killer compared himself to the shrek onion stuff, with his comparison being redacted. That… was really poor.

It would have been fine as a nonexistent murder. It would have been fine as a real anomalous murder that the foundation wants to cover up for some reason as nonexistent. It would have been fine as some sinister and destructive narrative element.

But the way it does what it does is just weak.

-1.

item.title: My reaction: "Meh."
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't really appeal to me. The basic premise is decent, but the specific wording is shoddy (read: not clinical enough) in certain spots and the overall structure relies too much on SCP-2747.

+ Critique and Suggestions
- Here goes...

The name "Evobasa Woleb" seems arbitrarily contrived to me, but I might be able to accept it if everything else is made better.

My main concern: This reads too much like SCP-2747. Above all other things in the article, that must be fixed. Here's basically how it reads:

Special Containment Procedures: What to do with it.
Description: What it is and what it does.
List of Affected Narratives: Contains copious amounts of foreshadowing.
The Big Reveal: Anomalously Lost

Not only is this more blatant than SCP-2747, it fails to capture my interest in quite the same way.
SCP-2747 has been referenced or implied in other articles before, but SCP-3775 barely has its own premise. You need to expand on the original ideas and make the SCP-2747 stuff less obvious. The best way to go about this is to hide the SCP-2747 narremes among everything else,
and go into more detail on the things unique to this particular anomaly. The memetic stuff seems promising to me.

Alternatively, you could decrease the drama leading up to the final [DATA CORRUPTED] and avoid SCP-2747 altogether, and instead make them related only tangentially.

In conclusion, change the structure, veer away from the SCP-2747 article to a certain degree, and beef up this article with meaningful information that further differentiates it from SCP-2747.
How exactly you go about doing this is up to you, author, but it must be done if you want the article to stand on its own.


Until this improves, I'm downvoting this, unironically bringing the vote count to 7.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That honestly hadn't occurred to me. I suppose I should've picked a different setting for the murder given the current political climate. *shrug*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the anomaly, but I wish it was tied to something other than a school (-related) shooting. This brings to mind "crisis actors" conspiracy theories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what's sillier? The fact that nobody documenting the phenomenon seems to notice it. And the fact that ogres don't have layers but ERROR - DATA CORRUPTED.

[Yeah, there's dumb stuff in this article, which may turn many people off. But there's a method to my madness]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm slightly biased because this is basically the last article I wrote, only more in-depth and better. <.<

That being said, the two things keeping me from an upvote are the strong tie to 2747 and the murderer's name being "Below Asabove" backwards. That's… I just don't know how to react to that, it's so obvious and silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy, I love me some pataphysics.

+1

Now that I've had time to think about it, this leans really heavily on SCP-2747 here. The structure of the two skips is nearly the same (test log of various media sources, followed by meta stuff), except it just doesn't have the same feel to it as SCP-2747. I don't know if it's the scattered DATA CORRUPTEDs, or the Shrek reference that just seems to come out of the left field, or what.

There is some merit here, like the implications of the murder that just up and vanished, but I would have liked it more if it was further explained what exactly happened to the murder. It might be because it's 7:30 and I'm too tired for this, though.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first attempt at a Keter in over four years, and also my 25th mainlist SCP. Shame I didn't think of this in time for the Jam Contest…ah well, coulda woulda shoulda.

+ [Spoilers]
- [Spoilers]

With apologies to minmin, who did this much better than I ever could.

The blackbox is "Pataphysics". The rest I leave to up to you.
